name: usbrip
summary: usbrip (inherited from "USB Ripper", not "USB R.I.P.") is a simple forensics tool with command line interface that lets you keep track of USB device artifacts (i.e., USB event history) on Linux machines.

documentation.readme: usbrip (inherited from "USB Ripper", not "USB R.I.P.") is a simple forensics tool with command line interface that lets you keep track of USB device artifacts (i.e., USB event history) on Linux machines.
usage_sections: Export collected data as a JSON dump for later use.
Generate a list of authorized (trusted) USB devices as a JSON file (call it auth.json).
Search for "violation events" based on auth.json: discover USB devices that do appear in history and do NOT appear in the auth.json file.

examples: Generate a list of trusted (authorized) USB devices.
Update storage -- add USB events to the existing storage. COMPRESSION_LEVEL is a number in [0..9].
Create storage -- create 7-Zip archive and add USB events to it according to the selected options.
Get extra details about a specific USB device by its <VID> and/or <PID> from the USB ID database.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with volatilityfoundation/volatility3 (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with simsong/bulk_extractor (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with omerbenamram/evtx (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: usbrip (inherited from "USB Ripper", not "USB R.I.P.") is a simple forensics tool with command line interface that lets you keep track of USB device artifacts (i.e., USB event history) on Linux machines.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: usbrip (inherited from "USB Ripper", not "USB R.I.P.") is a simple forensics tool with command line interface that lets you keep track of USB device artifacts (i.e., USB event history) on Linux machines.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `Get usbrip banner.`. Further invocations from upstream: `Get USB event history.`; `Open USB event dump.`. usbrip (inherited from "USB Ripper", not "USB R.I.P.") is a simple forensics tool with command line interface that lets you keep track of USB device artifacts (i.e., USB event history) on Linux machines.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Penetration Testing, use usbrip because usbrip (inherited from "USB Ripper", not "USB R.I.P.") is a simple forensics tool with command line interface that lets you keep track of USB device artifacts (.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Get usbrip banner.` as documented upstream for usbrip.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Get USB event history.` as documented upstream for usbrip.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Open USB event dump.` as documented upstream for usbrip.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Generate a list of trusted (authorized) USB devices.` as documented upstream for usbrip.
When chaining Penetration Testing tools, pair usbrip with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `usbrip --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

Update storage -- add USB events to the existing storage. COMPRESSION_LEVEL is a number in [0..9].
Create storage -- create 7-Zip archive and add USB events to it according to the selected options.
Get extra details about a specific USB device by its <VID> and/or <PID> from the USB ID database.
